meta.purpose: Draft mainstream behavior definition for IEG-100 Modbus data collection and MQTT publishing. This file is used for engineering planning, firmware validation, SEO claim control and future product page drafting.
meta.important_note: This is a planning baseline, not a confirmed production specification. Public product pages should use safe wording until firmware, UI and prototype tests validate device limits, polling performance and MQTT behavior.

product_scope.positioning: Ethernet-only baseline industrial IoT gateway for collecting Modbus RTU/TCP data and publishing it to MQTT brokers or cloud platforms.

field_interfaces.validation_status: isolation rating and bus protection require hardware validation

protocols: HTTP/HTTPS local web UI if implemented
field_interfaces.validation_status: final port count and web UI behavior require hardware and firmware validation

modbus_devices.safe_public_wording: Supports multiple Modbus devices after validation.
modbus_devices.notes: 32 slave devices is a practical first-generation target for a baseline gateway. Final number depends on polling interval, register count, baud rate and firmware memory.

data_points.safe_public_wording: Designed for hundreds of configured data points after validation.
data_points.notes: 512 points is a mainstream baseline target. Higher point counts should be reserved for IEG-E200 or later advanced edge gateways.

polling_tasks.definition: A polling task is one Modbus request block, such as reading contiguous holding registers from one slave.
polling_tasks.notes: Task grouping should reduce Modbus bus traffic by reading contiguous registers in blocks.

minimum_poll_interval.notes: 1 second should be treated as a best-case target for small point counts. Use 5 seconds as the default because it is more realistic for RS485 multi-device applications.

local_cache.target: 10000 latest records or 24 hours, whichever is reached first
local_cache.safe_public_wording: Store-and-forward buffering is planned and requires validation.
local_cache.notes: If local storage is removed from hardware, downgrade this feature to reconnect-only publishing without offline buffering.

roles.write_support_policy: Optional and disabled by default in UI; enable only for controlled output/write use cases.
Optional and disabled by default in UI.

error_handling.timeout: Mark affected points as stale and retry according to retry_count.
error_handling.crc_error: Discard frame, increment error counter and retry.
error_handling.illegal_function_or_address: Mark point configuration error and stop aggressive retries for that point.
error_handling.device_offline: Set device status to offline after 3 consecutive failed polling cycles.
error_handling.recovery: Set device status to online after 2 consecutive successful polling cycles.

authentication: CA certificate for TLS after validation
client certificate for mutual TLS after validation

qos.notes: QoS 1 is a practical default for industrial telemetry. QoS 2 is usually unnecessary for baseline gateways and adds complexity.

topic_rules: Use stable gateway_id, not IP address, in MQTT topics.
Do not include customer names or site names in default topics unless user-configured.
Keep telemetry on one default topic for simple cloud ingestion; advanced per-device or per-point topics can be added later.

telemetry_payload_format.timestamp: ISO 8601 UTC string preferred; Unix milliseconds optional for platforms that require it.
payload_rules: Every telemetry message must include gateway_id, timestamp and sequence number.
Every point must include id, value, quality and timestamp.
Unavailable or failed points should not be silently omitted; publish them with quality status when possible.
Large configurations may split payloads by device to keep MQTT messages small.

payload_size_target.notes: Split payloads when a polling cycle produces a larger message.

safety_rules: Commands must be disabled by default.
Each writable point must be explicitly enabled.
Command payload must include request_id, target device, point/register and value.
Gateway must publish a command response with success or error reason.
Do not enable public SEO claims for remote write/control until validation is complete.

configuration_validation_rules: Warn when polling interval is too short for configured device and point count.
Warn when a Modbus register block crosses an invalid address boundary.
Test MQTT broker connection before saving production configuration.
Test Modbus read for a selected point before enabling it.

local_diagnostics: Last read value and timestamp per point
log_targets: optional MQTT status/error topic after validation

test_cases.title: Modbus RTU polling basic read
test_cases.procedure: Configure one RS485 Modbus slave with FC03 and FC04 points and poll at 5 second interval.
test_cases.expected_result: Values are read correctly, scaled and published to MQTT with quality good.

test_cases.title: Modbus TCP polling basic read
test_cases.procedure: Configure one Modbus TCP device and poll FC03/FC04 registers.
test_cases.expected_result: Values are read correctly and published to MQTT.

test_cases.title: Multi-device polling
test_cases.procedure: Configure 32 Modbus devices or simulators with mixed polling tasks.
test_cases.expected_result: Gateway polls devices according to schedule without memory leak or task lockup.

test_cases.title: 512-point configuration
test_cases.procedure: Configure 512 data points across multiple devices and run a 24-hour stability test.
test_cases.expected_result: Gateway remains stable; telemetry publishes successfully; diagnostics remain usable.

test_cases.title: MQTT reconnect
test_cases.procedure: Disconnect broker network, then restore network.
test_cases.expected_result: Gateway reconnects using backoff policy and resumes publishing.

test_cases.title: Store-and-forward
test_cases.procedure: Disconnect MQTT broker while Modbus polling continues, then restore broker.
test_cases.expected_result: Cached records are published in order if local cache hardware is implemented; otherwise feature must remain unpublished.

test_cases.title: Payload schema
test_cases.procedure: Validate telemetry and status messages against the JSON payload rules.
test_cases.expected_result: Payload contains gateway_id, timestamp, sequence number, device status, point quality and values.

test_cases.title: Point error handling
test_cases.procedure: Configure invalid register address and offline slave.
test_cases.expected_result: Gateway marks affected points as config_error, read_error or offline; other points continue polling.

test_cases.title: MQTT QoS 1 delivery
test_cases.procedure: Publish telemetry using QoS 1 under unstable network conditions.
test_cases.expected_result: Gateway handles acknowledgements and reconnects without duplicated sequence confusion in downstream processing.

test_cases.title: Configuration import/export
test_cases.procedure: Export configuration, factory reset, import the configuration and restart polling.
test_cases.expected_result: Gateway restores Modbus devices, points and MQTT settings correctly.

allowed_before_validation: IEG-100 is planned as an Ethernet industrial IoT gateway for Modbus RTU/TCP data collection and MQTT publishing.
Designed for Modbus-to-MQTT applications such as energy meters, solar inverters, PLC-connected sensors and remote equipment monitoring.
MQTT JSON payload and local configuration workflow are under engineering validation.
not_allowed_before_validation: Exact maximum number of Modbus devices.
Exact maximum number of data points.
OPC UA, BACnet or CAN support on IEG-100.

next_actions: Confirm whether local storage is included for store-and-forward buffering.
Confirm whether TLS is included in first firmware release.
Create a JSON schema file for telemetry and status payloads if firmware team accepts this format.
Run GM-001 through GM-010 and record evidence in first-product-capability-validation-checklist.json.
